agents.name: Planner
agents.lastMessage: Introducing aromatic residues at position 5 and hydrophobic substitutions at pos

report.hypothesis: Introducing aromatic residues at position 5 and hydrophobic substitutions at position 11 will enhance SSTR2 selectivity by forming specific interactions with SSTR2-unique ECL2/ECL3/TM5/TM6 regions while reducing off-target affinity.

agents.name: Critic
agents.lastMessage: The current design prioritizes ddG but fails to achieve sufficient selectivity.

report.hypothesis: The current design prioritizes ddG but fails to achieve sufficient selectivity. The next iteration should focus on disrupting off-target interactions at non-pharmacophore positions while preserving the native pharmacophore to improve Δmargin.

proposed_changes.rationale: These positions are non-pharmacophore and are likely involved in off-target interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5. Focusing mutations here can disrupt off-target binding while preserving the native pharmacophore (FWKT(7-10) and Cys3/Cys14).
These mutations introduce charged or polar residues at non-pharmacophore positions to disrupt conserved pocket interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5, which are likely driving off-target binding.
